subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Let X be a gamma distribution with parameters $\alpha = 3$, and $\lambda = \frac{1}{6}$. (a) Give the density function, as well as the mean and standard deviation of X. (b) Find $E(3X^2 + X - 1)$.
answer: (a) The density function is $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{x^2}{432}e^{-x/6} & x\ge 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ $E(X) = 18$ and $\sigma = 10.39$ (b) 1313
explanation: Answer: (a) The density function is $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{x^2}{432}e^{-x/6} & x\ge 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ $E(X) = 18$ and $\sigma = 10.39$ (b) 1313
